what_it_does: Renders a fixed-position digital clock in the top-right corner that updates every second using a React effect interval.
observations: Uses useState and useEffect with setInterval/clearInterval
formatTime builds an HH:MM:SS string from the local Date
Styles are applied via an inline style object using CSS custom properties
The component name is lowercase live_clock_widget rather than PascalCase
zIndex is set to 9999 to float above other UI
standouts: Styling is fully driven by CSS variables for theming
Component identifier uses lowercase/snake_case instead of React PascalCase convention
concerns: Component name live_clock_widget is not PascalCase, which breaks React convention and may confuse tooling
No TypeScript types or props interface
No timezone or locale handling; relies on local system time
High zIndex of 9999 may clash with other overlays
notes: Small themed overlay clock widget; note the non-standard lowercase component naming.
questions: Is the lowercase component name intentional for dynamic registration or extension loading?
Where are the CSS custom properties such as --s0 and --tx defined?
intention_signals: Provide an always-visible, theme-aware live clock overlay without external dependencies.
contribution: A self-contained, interval-driven clock widget that demonstrates a simple React effect pattern and CSS-variable theming.
summary_for_embedding: A React functional component that renders a fixed-position digital clock updating every second via useEffect and setInterval, styled with CSS custom properties.
intention_for_embedding: To add a live time overlay to the canvas app that stays visible and themed.

symbols.does: Returns the current local time formatted as HH:MM:SS.
symbols.description: A local helper that creates a new Date, pads hours, minutes, and seconds to two digits, and returns them as a colon-separated string.

symbols.does: Renders a themed, fixed-position digital clock that refreshes every second.
symbols.description: A default-exported React functional component that initializes state with the formatted time, sets up a one-second interval in useEffect, and renders the time in a fixed top-right overlay styled with CSS variables.
